Title: ABA promotes fatty acid biosynthesis and transport to boost arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis in apple roots

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Fatty acid-derived lipid supply to arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Exports lipids from root cells to support AM fungal growth and arbuscule development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in mycorrhizal roots; increased by ABA and MdABF2; higher MdSTR-1 and MdSTR-2 transcript levels in MdSTR2-OE roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizophagus irregularis inoculation; exogenous ABA; fluridone treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression promoted AM symbiosis and arbuscule formation without significantly altering total FA or TAG content, indicating a transport role rather than synthetic role
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy-root overexpression/silencing; AM phenotyping; BiFC showing homodimerization; EMSA/GUS/dual-luciferase/ChIP-qPCR showing regulation by MdABF2 and MdWRI3
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major growth effect without AM inoculation reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful transporter target to enhance lipid export and mycorrhizal performance without necessarily increasing bulk FA accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdSTR2 is induced during AM symbiosis and transcriptionally activated by MdABF2 directly and by MdWRI3 indirectly. 2. MdSTR2 forms homodimers in apple. 3. MdSTR2 overexpression enhances AM colonization and arbuscule formation, likely by increasing lipid transport rather than synthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains both ABRE and AW-box motifs, consistent with dual control by MdABF2 and MdWRI3.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Marker of active AM symbiosis and phosphate uptake interface
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression strongly induced during AM colonization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdPT4 expression was strongly induced by AM fungi. 2. The paper uses MdPT4 mainly as a symbiosis-associated marker rather than a direct causal nutrient-metabolism gene.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is experimentally monitored in Results, but it is not a primary mechanistic focus relative to MdNCED3.1/3.2, MdABF2, MdKASIII, and MdSTR2.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative cleavage step of carotenoid-derived ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, a direct precursor in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fatty acid biosynthesis and transport in roots, enhancing lipid supply to arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi and supporting AM symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in roots after AM fungal inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizophagus irregularis inoculation; exogenous ABA; fluridone treatment
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies signaling metabolite that activates downstream fatty acid synthesis/transport program
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases ABA content and enhances AM fungal colonization and arbuscule formation; RNAi reduces ABA accumulation and suppresses colonization, partially rescued by exogenous ABA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy-root overexpression and RNAi in apple; ABA quantification; mycorrhizal colonization and arbuscule phenotyping; exogenous ABA rescue
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Non-inoculated overexpression lines showed stunted shoot growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Increasing root ABA biosynthesis via MdNCED3.1 may improve AM colonization and nutrient acquisition, but constitutive elevation may impair growth without symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AM fungal colonization strongly induced MdNCED3.1 expression in apple roots. 2. MdNCED3.1 overexpression increased ABA accumulation and promoted AM colonization and arbuscule development. 3. MdNCED3.1 silencing reduced ABA and impaired symbiosis, and exogenous ABA partially rescued the phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of MdABF2-mediated regulation of fatty acid biosynthesis and transport genes.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative cleavage step of carotenoid-derived ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to generate xanthoxin for ABA production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fatty acid biosynthesis and transport in roots, enhancing lipid supply to arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi and supporting AM symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in roots after AM fungal inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizophagus irregularis inoculation; exogenous ABA; fluridone treatment
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies signaling metabolite that activates downstream fatty acid synthesis/transport program
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases ABA content and enhances AM fungal colonization and arbuscule formation; RNAi reduces ABA accumulation and suppresses colonization, partially rescued by exogenous ABA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy-root overexpression and RNAi in apple; ABA quantification; mycorrhizal colonization and arbuscule phenotyping; exogenous ABA rescue
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Non-inoculated overexpression lines showed stunted shoot growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Increasing root ABA biosynthesis via MdNCED3.2 may improve AM colonization and nutrient acquisition, but constitutive elevation may impair growth without symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AM fungal colonization strongly induced MdNCED3.2 expression in apple roots. 2. MdNCED3.2 overexpression increased ABA accumulation and promoted AM colonization and arbuscule development. 3. MdNCED3.2 silencing reduced ABA and impaired symbiosis, and exogenous ABA partially rescued the phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of MdABF2-mediated regulation of fatty acid biosynthesis and transport genes.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Plastid de novo fatty acid initiation module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the initial condensation of acetyl-CoA with malonyl-ACP to initiate fatty acid chain synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Fatty acids and triacylglycerol-derived lipid supply for AM fungus
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provide carbon and lipid precursors supporting fungal growth, colonization, and arbuscule development during AM symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in mycorrhizal roots; promoted by ABA and MdABF2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizophagus irregularis inoculation; exogenous ABA; fluridone treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased FA and TAG contents and enhanced AM colonization and arbuscule formation; RNAi decreased FA/TAG and reduced colonization, partially rescued by exogenous ABA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy-root overexpression and RNAi; FA and TAG quantification; AM colonization phenotyping; promoter binding and activation by MdABF2 via EMSA, GUS, dual-luciferase, ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduced seedling growth under non-inoculated conditions; overexpression improved growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enhancing MdKASIII expression may strengthen root lipid provisioning and improve AM symbiosis, potentially aiding nutrient uptake
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdKASIII is induced in mycorrhizal roots and positively regulated by ABA/MdABF2. 2. MdABF2 directly binds and activates the MdKASIII promoter. 3. MdKASIII overexpression raises FA and TAG contents and promotes AM symbiosis, whereas silencing has the opposite effect.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents a core FA synthesis target in the ABA-MdABF2 pathway.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: fatty acid biosynthesis and lipid transport during AM symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates transcription of MdKASIII, MdWRI3, and MdSTR2; also required for induction of additional lipid-related genes including MdKASI, MdKASI-1, MdRAM2, and MdRAM2-1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ABA-responsive regulator linking ABA accumulation to root fatty acid synthesis/transport and AM fungal colonization
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Fatty acids and lipid supply to arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provide carbon to AM fungi, supporting colonization and arbuscule development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in roots after AM inoculation, after exogenous ABA treatment, and in MdNCED3.1/3.2 overexpression lines; accumulates in arbuscule-containing cells
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA accumulation induced by AM fungal infection; Rhizophagus irregularis colonization
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizophagus irregularis inoculation; exogenous ABA; fluridone treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression enhanced root growth, AM colonization, and arbuscule formation; RNAi reduced arbuscule formation and lowered FA/TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy-root overexpression and RNAi; promoter:GUS localization; Y1H; EMSA; GUS reporter assay; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; FA/TAG profiling; mycorrhizal phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported; overexpression improved growth under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising regulator for engineering enhanced AM symbiosis by coordinately activating root lipid synthesis and export pathways
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdABF2 is induced by AM fungi and ABA and accumulates in arbuscule-containing cells. 2. MdABF2 directly binds promoters of MdKASIII, MdWRI3, and MdSTR2 and activates their expression. 3. MdABF2 overexpression increases FA/TAG accumulation and AM colonization, while silencing reduces arbuscule formation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves as the central transcriptional mediator of ABA-promoted lipid provisioning to AM fungi.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: fatty acid transport / lipid transfer module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MdSTR2 transcription by binding the AW-box-containing MdSTR2 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as an intermediate downstream regulator connecting MdABF2 to lipid transporter expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Fatty acid-derived lipid supply to arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports lipid transfer from root cells to AM fungi for fungal development
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: M. hupehensis Rhed; tobacco leaves for transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in apple roots and strongly upregulated after AM inoculation; induced in an MdABF2-dependent manner
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes expression of the lipid transporter gene MdSTR2, supporting fatty acid transport during symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; EMSA; GUS reporter assay; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be stacked with MdABF2 or transporter engineering to enhance root lipid export to AM fungi
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdWRI3 is an MdABF2-activated transcription factor induced in mycorrhizal roots. 2. MdWRI3 directly binds the MdSTR2 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. MdWRI3 provides an indirect route by which ABA/MdABF2 enhances lipid transport to AM fungi.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional evidence in this paper centers on promoter regulation rather than direct transgenic phenotyping of MdWRI3.
